libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: No environment variables or system properties are required. If preserving the existing `IterablesTest`, update it to the current 3.1.0 API/package (`org.codehaus.commons.compiler.util.iterator.Iterables`) rather than adding a dependency. Avoid covering optional Ant integration unless intentionally adding Ant-specific tests.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8855 [Automation] javac compile fails for org.codehaus.janino:janino:3.1.0; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015

risks: Janino 3.1.0 moved iterator utilities out of `org.codehaus.janino.util.iterator`; stale imports from 3.0.16 will fail javac.
The Janino artifact declares Apache Ant as optional/provided for Ant adapter integration; core compiler usage does not require it.
Janino generates and loads classes dynamically, so native-image runtime behavior may still require tests to tolerate unsupported dynamic class loading, as the existing scaffold already does.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: No extra setup is needed. `org.codehaus.janino:janino:3.1.0` transitively brings `org.codehaus.janino:commons-compiler:3.1.0`; the observed compile failure is caused by a package/API move of `Iterables`, not by a missing dependency, Docker service, or environment setting.
